title: onobrychis sativa subsp. montana
intro: Onobrychis sativa subsp. montana, also known as Alpine Sainfoin or Mountain Sainfoin, is a subspecies of the Sainfoin plant, which is a perennial legume native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Onobrychis sativa subsp. montana include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
